finding_results.notes: Replaced the old-race-clips beat (mother pausing footage on Kai's step out of the blocks to locate the 'real' pre-rebuild Kai) with a phone-call beat in which Kai's mother inventories old bodily complaints — a popping knee, a calf cramp in rain — that no longer apply because the joints and muscles were replaced during the rebuild. The new beat serves the same emotional function (mother searching for the recognizable version of her son) through a different concrete cue (cataloguing vanished physical defects rather than comparing performance media). No footage, clips, or before-and-after media comparison remains. The transition into Lena's 'And are you' at line 195 is preserved and strengthened: Lena looks at the wrist brace (a current injury) after hearing about pain that no longer exists, and the implicit question shifts from 'are you the person in the old video' to 'are you the person whose body still hurts in ways your mother would recognize.' Kai's voice maintained: high contractions, Midwestern diction, indirect approach to emotional weight through physical detail, self-correction through addition rather than retraction.

summary: Single finding addressed. The style-bible-echo beat where Kai's mother sends old race clips to search for the pre-rebuild version of him has been replaced with a different maternal cue: she asks about specific old bodily complaints (a synthetic knee that no longer pops, a replaced calf that no longer cramps) and Kai reports that she moves silently to the next item when he says each one is gone. The replacement preserves the same fear — a mother trying to locate her son inside a changed body — without relying on footage, clips, or recorded performance media. No other dialogue, voice signatures, or chapter structure was altered; the heading contract, setup obligations (kai_override_training), and boundary continuity are intact.
